author: Robert Carr
description: A simple egg salad sandwich with chopped dill pickles, onion, lettuce or arugula, and a Dijon mayo dressing on wheat bread or ciabatta.
ingredients: 2 slices wheat sandwich bread or 1 small ciabatta roll, toasted if desired
1/4 cup chopped dill pickles, patted dry
Small handful lettuce or baby arugula, patted dry
A few very thin onion slices, minced if you want them in the egg salad
1 teaspoon pickle brine from the jar
instructions: Place the eggs in a small saucepan and cover them with water by about 1 inch.
Bring the water to a boil over medium-high heat, then turn off the heat, cover the pan, and let the eggs sit for 10 minutes.
Move the eggs to cold water for 2 minutes, then peel them.
Chop the eggs and place them in a bowl.
Add the mayonnaise, Dijon mustard, pickle brine, chopped pickles, and minced onion if using onion in the salad.
Mash with a fork until the egg salad is creamy but still a little chunky.
Toss the lettuce or arugula with the extra-virgin olive oil.
Spread the egg salad on the bread, add the dressed greens, close the sandwich, and cut in half.
notes: Pickles, mayo, and Dijon give the egg salad a tangy, creamy base without needing meat.
Pat the chopped pickles dry before mixing so the egg salad tastes tangy without becoming watery.
Use ciabatta if you want more chew and crunch; use wheat sandwich bread if you want a softer, easier lunch.
If you already have hard-boiled eggs, this becomes a 5-minute sandwich.
title: Dill Pickle Egg Salad Sandwich
